requirements.summary: Display average rating on VendorCard/VendorDirectoryPage using existing review data
acceptanceCriteria: Each vendor card in the VendorDirectoryPage displays an average star rating (1–5) computed from their reviews
If a vendor has no reviews, display a 'No reviews yet' indicator instead of a rating
Rating is visually represented with filled/empty stars or a numeric badge consistent with the existing design system

file_operations.description: Create a VendorCard component that displays vendor information (name, bio, categories) with an inline average rating badge. Use the useVendorRatingSummary hook to fetch rating data for the vendor's principal ID. Show star icons (filled/empty) or a numeric badge representing the average rating. If reviewCount is 0, display 'No reviews yet' text instead of stars. Keep styling consistent with the existing ProductCard and DAppCard components using Tailwind classes.

Replace the existing vendor list rendering to use the new VendorCard component for each vendor. Pass each vendor's data to VendorCard. Ensure the VendorCard is clickable and navigates to the vendor's store page. Maintain existing search, filter, and sort functionality. The VendorCard will handle rating display internally via its own hook calls.

requirements.summary: Add average rating and review count to VendorStorePage header
acceptanceCriteria: VendorStorePage header/hero section shows the vendor's average star rating and total review count
Data is derived from the same reviews query already used by VendorReviewsSection
Display updates reactively when new reviews are submitted

file_operations.description: Add the useVendorRatingSummary hook call at the top of the component, passing the vendorId from route params. In the vendor header/hero section (before the products grid), add a rating display element showing average star rating and review count (e.g., '4.5 ★ (23 reviews)'). Use star icons consistent with VendorCard. Ensure the rating display appears prominently near the vendor's display name and bio. The data will reactively update when React Query invalidates the rating summary query after new review submission.

requirements.summary: Expose getVendorRatingSummary backend query as useVendorRatingSummary React Query hook
acceptanceCriteria: useVendorRatingSummary hook is added to frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts
Hook accepts a vendorId parameter and returns averageRating and reviewCount
Hook is consumed by VendorDirectoryPage vendor cards and VendorStorePage header

file_operations.description: Add a new useVendorRatingSummary hook that accepts vendorId (string) as parameter. Use React Query's useQuery with queryKey ['vendorRatingSummary', vendorId]. Call actor.getReviewsAggregate(vendorId) in the queryFn (the backend already provides this aggregate function). Return the averageRating and count fields from the ReviewsAggregate response. Enable the query only when actor is available and vendorId is non-empty. Set staleTime to 60000ms to allow caching across multiple vendor cards.

requirements.summary: Add sortable 'Rating' option to VendorDirectoryPage sort controls
acceptanceCriteria: VendorDirectoryPage sort controls include a 'Top Rated' or 'Rating' option alongside existing name/date sorts
Selecting 'Rating' sorts vendors descending by average rating
Sort option syncs to URL params consistent with existing sort param handling

file_operations.description: Add a new sort option 'rating' to the existing sortVendors utility function. For the rating sort, accept a Map<string, number> parameter containing vendorId-to-averageRating mappings. Sort vendors descending by their average rating (highest first). If a vendor has no rating in the map, treat it as 0. Keep existing 'name' and 'createdAt' sort logic unchanged. Export a type union for valid sort keys including 'rating'.

Add 'Top Rated' to the sort dropdown options in the controls section. Read the sort param from URL using the existing urlParams utility. For each vendor, call useVendorRatingSummary to build a vendorId-to-rating map (handle this efficiently to avoid excessive hook calls; consider using a single aggregate query or useMemo). Pass the rating map to the sortVendors utility when sortBy is 'rating'. Update the URL param when the user selects 'Top Rated'. Ensure the sort dropdown reflects the current URL param on page load.
